description_long: This is a short wooden picket fence section, constructed from five vertical pickets with pointed tops, evenly spaced and attached to two horizontal rails. The wood appears to be light in color, possibly pine or cedar, and is fastened together with visible metallic bolts or screws at each intersection. The pickets are flat and rectangular, with the tops cut into a simple pointed design, giving the fence a classic suburban or garden appearance. The rails are set behind the pickets, providing structural support and rigidity. The overall finish is matte, with a natural wood grain texture visible across all surfaces. This type of fence is commonly used for decorative or low-security boundary marking in residential yards, parks, or urban green spaces.
description: A short, light-colored wooden picket fence with five pointed pickets and two horizontal rails, fastened by visible metal bolts. The design is classic and simple, suitable for residential or garden boundaries.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the thinness of the fence and the alignment of the pickets and rails.
description_view_1: Front view displaying five pointed pickets, two horizontal rails, and visible metal fasteners.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, similar to view 0, highlighting the fence's slim structure.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat backs of the pickets and the two horizontal rails more clearly.
